refutationAttempt: Tried to argue the MB tracklist is incomplete so the keyTrack could still exist on the release.
decision: Search match (sim 1.0) on title/artist/year/label with a complete-looking 10-track listing; three of four keyTracks are present in that list while 'Tell Me a Bedtime Story' is absent, so the same release demonstrably lacks the claimed track.
contradictingQuote: ["Rising Son", "Afro Blues", "Piri Piri", "Mala", "Everybody Loves the Sunshine", "Green and Gold", "Sometime, Somewhere, Somehow", "Call", "Unchain", "Promise in Love"]

refutationAttempt: Tried to read 'Mandorla Island' as the same track as 'Mandorla Awakening', and to treat the search match as possibly incomplete.
decision: Search match (sim 1.0) on title/artist/label/year; the 10-track list contains 'Mandorla Island' (a distinct title) and 'Egoes War'/'Listening Embrace' but neither 'Mandorla Awakening' nor 'Ceremony of Infinite Projection', so two claimed keyTracks are absent from the same release.
contradictingQuote: ["Egoes War", "Sub-Mission", "The Chalice", "Dance of Many Hands", "Listening Embrace", "Forestwall Timewalk", "Staircase Struggle", "Shiny Divider", "Mandorla Island", "Timewrap"]

refutationAttempt: Tried to defend '2018' as a possible recording-vs-release gap and 'his Masada ensemble' as loose framing of a Zorn-composed series.
decision: Embedded-MBID (authoritative) release is dated 2019-04, directly contradicting the prose's explicit '2018 album'; the credited performers are Julian Lage and Gyan Riley, not a Masada ensemble. Right album, specific wrong fact -> factual_error stands.

refutationAttempt: Tried the editorial-style defense that naming a co-creator (Danilo Perez) is legitimate even if MB credits another leader.
decision: Embedded-MBID (authoritative for artist credit) credits the release to Kurt Elling; the prose asserts 'pianist Danilo Perez leads' this exact release (same title/label/date, all five keyTracks match). The leadership attribution contradicts the authoritative credit -> factual_error.

refutationAttempt: Tried to treat 'Jorge Pedron' as a benign variant and the 'guitarist' descriptor as unverifiable.
decision: Embedded-MBID release credits 'Pierrick Pedron' (corroborated by the stored Rubalcaba Wikipedia discography line 'Pedron Rubalcaba with Pierrick Pedron'); the prose invents the name 'Jorge Pedron'. The first-name contradiction alone is a factual error on the same album.

refutationAttempt: Tried to argue the Grammy sentence could coincidentally also describe Metheny.
decision: The stored Wikipedia page is Scott Colley's, and its extract carries the identical sentence verbatim about Colley's four Grammy nominations for 'Guided Tour' and 'Still Dreaming' -- a different person's biography grafted into Metheny's blurb. Right album entity, one foreign factual claim -> factual_error.
contradictingQuote: Scott Colley (born November 24, 1963) is an American jazz double bassist and composer. As of 2024, he had been nominated for 4 Grammy Awards, including Best Jazz Instrumental Album for Guided Tour in 2014 and Still Dreaming in 2019.
specificBadClaim: As of 2024, he had been nominated for 4 Grammy Awards, including Best Jazz Instrumental Album for Guided Tour in 2014 and Still Dreaming in 2019.

refutationAttempt: Tried to argue ours might be a different 1988 'Carolina Shout' compilation, so the missing tracks could live elsewhere.
decision: Exact title and year (1988) match the Biograph release and two distinctive keyTracks ('Carolina Shout' and 'Harlem Strut') are present in its 14-track list, tying ours to this release; the other four keyTracks (Backwater Blues, Snowy Morning Blues, Chicago Blues, My Handy Man) are absent from that same list.
contradictingQuote: ["Steeplechase Rag", "Twilight Rag", "Carolina Shout", "Baltimore Buzz", "Gypsy Blues", "Harlem Strut", "Eccentricity", "Don't Mess With Me", "Nervous Blues", "Ole Miss Blues", "I Ain't Givin' Nothin' Away", "Muscle Shoal Blues", "Farewell Blues", "Charleston"]

refutationAttempt: Adversarial read: the matched MB release is a different compilation, so the missing tracks may simply be on ours.
decision: The match is a search hit on a generic comp title with a DIFFERENT year (MB 2001 vs ours 1992), different label (Rajon vs ours' placeholder) and different country -- the evidence does not establish this is ours' actual release. Absence of two tracks from an unconfirmed different compilation is not a contradiction of ours. The judge itself conceded the two tracks 'could simply be on the 1992 edition.'

refutationAttempt: Tried to argue the 40-track listing might be incomplete and omit the famous sides.
decision: Embedded-MBID (authoritative, same release; year 2005 matches MB 2005-03-07) yields a detailed 40-track listing in which none of the six keyTracks -- not even the title track 'Begin the Beguine' -- appears. On the confirmed same release, all six claimed keyTracks are absent.
contradictingQuote: ["The Japanese Sandman", "A Pretty Girl I Like a Melody", "I Used to Be Above Love", "No Regrets", "South Sea Island Magic", "It Ain't Right", "Sugar Foot Stomp", "Thou Swell", "You're Giving Me a Song and a Dance", "Darling, Not Without You", "One, Two, Button Your Shoe", "Let's Call a Heart a Heart", "The Skeleton in the Closet", "There's Something in the Air", "Take Another Guess", "There's Frost on the Moon", "Love and Learn", "Moon Face", "The Same Old Line", "You Can Tell She Comes From Dixie", "Sobbin' Blues", "Copenhagen", "Cream Puff", "My Blue Heaven", "Streamline", "Sweet Lorraine", "Love Is Good for Anything That Ails You", "No More Tears", "Moonlight and Shadows", "Was It Rain?", "All Alone", "All God's Chillun Got Rhythm", "It Goes to Your Feet", "Because I Love You", "Night and Day", "I Surrender Dear", "Blues Skies", "Someday, Sweetheart", "Afraid to Dream", "If You Should Ever Leave"]

refutationAttempt: Tried to defend '2008' as a possible reissue/recording year distinct from the matched 1974 release.
decision: All six keyTracks match the matched release's tracklist, so it is unambiguously the same release; both the release and release-group are dated 1974. A 1974 Columbia compilation cannot be 'Recorded in 2008', and no 2008 release appears anywhere in the evidence -> factual_error on the year.
specificBadClaim: Recorded in 2008, Hi De Ho Man

refutationAttempt: Adversarial read: ours is a different (third) 'Body and Soul' Hawkins compilation, so absence from the two matched comps doesn't prove ours' tracklist wrong.
decision: Ours' year is null and the MB hit is only a search match on a generic comp title (Dreyfus 2001); neither the MB tracklist nor the separate 1996 Wikipedia comp is confirmed to be ours' actual release. The six keyTracks form a coherent real Hawkins program (the Paris 'Crazy Rhythm'/'Honeysuckle Rose' sides), consistent with yet another same-titled comp. Absence from two unconfirmed title-matches is not a contradiction of ours.

refutationAttempt: Adversarial read: the matched release is explicitly 'Vol. 2' while ours omits the volume, so the wrong volume was matched.
decision: MB entityMatch is 'unsure'; the search matched 'Django And His American Friends, Vol. 2' but ours' title has no volume and the judge concluded ours 'looks like Vol. 1.' If a different volume was matched, ours' keyTracks could be correct for ours' actual (unmatched) release, so their absence from the Vol. 2 tracklist is not a contradiction.

refutationAttempt: Tried to argue the long alt-take listing might still be missing the famous tunes.
decision: Embedded-MBID (authoritative, same release; year 2020 matches) gives a 25-track listing containing 'Now's The Time' but none of the other five keyTracks (A Night in Tunisia, Ornithology, Parker's Mood, Relaxin' at Camarillo, Bird of Paradise). On the confirmed same release, five of six claimed keyTracks are absent.
contradictingQuote: ["The Song Is You", "Laird Baird", "Kim", "Kim [alt tk]", "Cosmic Rays", "Cosmic Rays [alt tk]", "Chi Chi", "Chi Chi [alt tk1]", "Chi Chi [alt tk2]", "Chi Chi [alt tk3]", "I Remember You", "Now's The Time", "Confirmation", "I'm in the Mood for Love*", "Star Eyes*", "Blues [Fast]*", "Celebrity*", "Ballade*", "Si Si*", "Swedish Schnapps*", "Swedish Schnapps [alt tk]*", "Back Home Blues*", "Back Home Blues [alt tk]*", "Lover Man*", "Blues for Alice*"]

refutationAttempt: Tried to defend '2022' as a reissue year and to discount the discarded MB single match.
decision: The entity-matched Wikipedia 'Afro (album)' (all six keyTracks match its tracklist) states the album was released in November 1954; a 1954 studio album cannot be 'Recorded in 2022' and no 2022 release appears in the evidence -> factual_error on the year.
contradictingQuote: It was released in November 1954 through Norgran Records

refutationAttempt: Tried to defend '1996' as a possible CD-reissue year.
decision: The entity-matched Wikipedia page (all five keyTracks match) gives a recording date of March 9, 1956 and calls it a 1956 album; the matched MB release is 1987. 'Recorded in 1996' contradicts the documented 1956 recording date and is supported by nothing in the evidence -> factual_error on the year.

refutationAttempt: Tried to defend '2011' as a CD-reissue year (Wikipedia mentions a CD reissue).
decision: Both MB and the entity-matched Wikipedia page (all six keyTracks match) date the album to 1963 on Verve; Wikipedia names only a 1997 CD reissue, not 2011. 'Recorded in 2011' contradicts the 1963 release/recording and has no evidence support -> factual_error on the year.

refutationAttempt: Tried to argue the matched release's own date is 1993-04-20, so '1993' could be defensible.
decision: Embedded-MBID release is a Live album whose release-group first appeared 1957-07; the 1993-04-20 release is a reissue and all six keyTracks match. A live recording first issued in July 1957 cannot have been 'Recorded in 1993' -> factual_error on the year.

refutationAttempt: Tried to argue '1997' matches the matched release date so it could be valid.
decision: Embedded-MBID release is a Live album literally titled 'Joe Pass at the Montreux Jazz Festival 1975' with release-group first release 1975; the 1997-05-16 release is the OJC reissue and all six keyTracks match. A 1975 live recording cannot be 'Recorded in 1997' -> factual_error on the year.
contradictingQuote: Joe Pass at the Montreux Jazz Festival 1975

refutationAttempt: Tried to defend '1993' as a reissue year and to treat the search match as a possibly different release.
decision: Search match on Chet Baker & Crew with five of six keyTracks matching exactly (the sixth differs by one letter, 'Lisa' vs 'Liza') ties ours to this Pacific Jazz release dated 1956 (release and release-group). A 1956 album cannot be 'Recorded in 1993' and no 1993 release appears in evidence -> factual_error on the year.
specificBadClaim: Recorded in 1993, Chet Baker and Crew

refutationAttempt: Tried to defend '2000' as a CD-reissue year distinct from the recording.
decision: Embedded-MBID release has release-group first release 1957-01-01 on Contemporary and all six keyTracks match; the matched 2000 release is a CD reissue. A 1957 album cannot be 'Recorded in 2000' -> factual_error on the year.
